When the narrator's clothes were causing him to sink, Counsel offered to cut them off. Then he used a knife to cut the clothes from top to bottom and removed them quickly while the narrator swam for both of them. After that, the narrator did the same for Counsel. So, the main reason for cutting the clothes seems to be that their clothes were becoming a burden. The clothes were heavy when wet, making it difficult for them to swim and stay afloat. By cutting off the clothes, they could remove that weight and swim more easily. Looking at the options: A) Because no people helped them to take off their clothes.
